class.name: AutoworkStubber
class.experimental: Some methods may not be implemented and may be changed in future versions.
class.brief_description: Defines and manages stubbed method behavior for test doubles.
class.description:
The AutoworkStubber is responsible for overriding method behavior during tests by defining custom return values.
It allows tests to replace real method implementations with controlled responses, enabling deterministic and isolated testing. Stubs can be defined globally for a method or conditionally based on arguments.

method.name: clear
method.description:
Clears all defined stubs.
After calling this method, no methods will have overridden return values and all behavior will revert to default implementation.

method.name: get_return
method.description:
Returns the stubbed return value for a method call.
If a stub exists for the given [param object], [param method_name], and optional [param args], the configured return value is returned. Otherwise, [code]null[/code] is returned or the original method behavior is used depending on context.

method.name: set_return
method.description:
Defines a stubbed return value for a method.
When [param method_name] is called on [param object], the provided [param return_value] will be returned instead of executing the original method. If [param args] is specified, the stub only applies when the arguments match.

method.name: should_call_super
method.description:
Returns whether the original method implementation should still be called.
If [code]true[/code], the stub allows execution of the original method in addition to or instead of returning a stubbed value. This is useful for partial stubbing or wrapping behavior.
